name: Behat
description: Open-source Behaviour-Driven Development framework for PHP using Gherkin feature files, the PHP equivalent of Cucumber/JBehave.

seo.title: Behat — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: Behat: Open-source Behaviour-Driven Development framework for PHP using Gherkin feature files, the PHP equivalent of Cucumber/JBehave.

when_to_use: BDD acceptance tests written in Gherkin for PHP apps
Business-readable feature files shared with stakeholders
API context tests for Symfony and Laravel backends
Automate user journey scenarios in headless browsers

pros: Reuses existing PHP unit test infrastructure
cons: Gherkin adds indirection few BAs actually read
Step-definition maintenance costs creep over time
Browser automation via Mink is increasingly dated
Slower feedback loop than plain PHPUnit
review.verdict: Classic PHP BDD framework that pays off only when product and QA truly collaborate on feature files.
review.best_for: PHP teams with real three-amigos workflow who share specs with the business
review.not_for: Dev-only teams where Gherkin is just extra ceremony over plain PHPUnit tests
